description_long: This object is a heavily weathered and partially destroyed airplane fuselage, with significant structural damage and missing sections. The exterior is primarily a faded, off-white color with patches of rust and darkened areas, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements. The fuselage is hollow, with large holes and jagged edges along the sides and ends, revealing the empty interior. The wings and tail assembly are missing, leaving only the central body of the aircraft. The surface is rough and uneven, with visible dents, tears, and corrosion. The overall appearance is that of an abandoned or crashed airplane wreck, stripped of most functional components.
description: A damaged, hollow airplane fuselage with missing wings and tail, featuring a rough, corroded surface and large holes.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated, hollow fuselage with jagged openings and a faded, patchy surface.
description_view_1: Front view revealing the open, empty nose section and the extent of internal damage and corrosion.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting more jagged edges, large holes, and the overall battered condition.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the open, torn end of the fuselage and the hollow interior.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Wings are missing; no visible configuration or design elements present.
attribute_engine_type: No engines visible on the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Tail assembly is missing; no tail configuration present.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear is absent or not visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward section of the fuselage is present, but cockpit details are missing or destroyed; no canopy or windows visible.
